patient.name: Jessica Thompson
symptoms: difficulty bearing weight on the foot
patient.medical_history: Previous ankle sprain last year, no surgeries

decision.triage_reasoning: The patient is experiencing symptoms related to an ankle injury, including swelling, bruising, and difficulty bearing weight. The mild nature of the pain and the absence of severe symptoms suggest a semi-urgent need for care.
decision.notes: Patient has a history of previous ankle sprain but no surgeries or critical conditions reported. Current medications do not indicate any chronic issues.
decision.context_for_next: Patient requires an evaluation to assess the extent of the ankle injury and to rule out fractures.
Due to the ankle injury symptoms and absence of any completed tests, additional imaging is needed to rule out a fracture and assess the injury.
decision.reasoning: The patient exhibits signs of a possible ankle injury, including swelling and difficulty bearing weight. Given the need to rule out fractures and considering the semi-urgent nature of the assessment, an ankle X-ray is warranted to evaluate for any bone abnormalities. The previous experiences suggest the necessity of imaging in cases of joint injuries. No tests have been completed yet, making this step essential.

decision.test_outcome: Fracture present in the fibula, joint effusion noted, no other acute abnormality.

decision.recommended_treatment: Orthopedic consultation for fracture management, possible immobilization or surgery, depending on the severity and displacement of the fracture. Follow-up X-ray may be needed to assess healing progress.
decision.context_for_next: Patient has a fibula fracture confirmed by X-ray; orthopedic evaluation is pertinent for further management.
decision.reasoning: The X-ray results indicate a fracture in the fibula along with joint effusion. This aligns with the symptoms of ankle injury suggesting semi-urgent care is needed. Treatment should focus on orthopedic management of the fracture, which is consistent with the similar cases found in past experiences. Tests completed: 1 (X-ray)

final_diagnosis.recommended_treatment: Orthopedic consultation for fracture management, possible immobilization or surgery, depending on the severity and displacement of the fracture. Follow-up X-ray may be needed to assess healing progress.
final_diagnosis.context_for_next: Patient has a fibula fracture confirmed by X-ray; orthopedic evaluation is pertinent for further management.
